beats.activity: Wake up, shower, make instant ramen and energy drink for breakfast
beats.reasoning: Jin is a night owl who skips morning classes, so waking at 10am is realistic. Folds morning routine into one beat to avoid back-to-back home beats.

beats.activity: Walk to nearby cafe or common area to watch FIFA match (USA vs Wales at 11am); Watch USA vs Wales FIFA World Cup match
beats.reasoning: Jin lives on energy and campus culture; the 11am FIFA match is timely and Jin would catch it. Light Rail closure doesn't affect this local walk.

beats.activity: Grab lunch at a campus food spot
beats.location_name: HUB Food Court (University of Washington, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Natural lunch break after the match. Still on campus, no travel needed.

beats.activity: Head to library for afternoon study session; Afternoon study session and CS coursework
beats.location_name: University of Washington Libraries (University of Washington, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Jin studies at the library regularly. Light Rail closure is irrelevant since already on campus.

beats.activity: Walk to IMA for pickup basketball game; Play pickup basketball with other students
beats.location_name: IMA (Intramural Activities Building, University of Washington, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Jin plays pickup basketball regularly. Light Rail maintenance has cleared by 3pm, so no transit issues. Local campus walk.

beats.activity: Walk back to U-District and grab dinner at a casual spot
beats.reasoning: Post-exercise hunger and social energy. The Ave has many affordable options Jin would frequent as a student.

beats.activity: Return home and gaming session with energy drinks
beats.reasoning: Jin is a gamer and night owl. Evening gaming is core to personality. Commute time from The Ave back to home.

beats.location_name: University of Washington Libraries (University of Washington, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Jin studies at the library until 2am regularly; this is a night-owl study beat. Walk/commute back to campus from home.

beats.activity: Sleep after returning home from late-night study
beats.reasoning: Jin returns home and sleeps. Beat spans midnight and ends by 6am as required. Winds down at home before sleep.

diary: Woke up to perfect weather and honestly felt like the universe was cooperating. Made my usual ramen setup and headed to U-District Commons to catch the USA-Wales match—the place was packed with people yelling at the screen, and I got caught up in the energy even though I'm not usually a huge soccer fan. Grabbed some mediocre food at the HUB afterward because I was starving, then spent the afternoon in the library grinding through some CS assignments that have been piling up. The Light Rail closure meant I had to walk to IMA, but that actually felt good. Basketball was exactly what I needed—got sweaty, played decent, forgot about code for a couple hours. Grabbed dinner on The Ave with some cash I had left, and then came home for some gaming to decompress. Ended up back at the library later because I couldn't shake the feeling I was behind on work. Got home pretty late feeling exhausted but oddly satisfied.
thought_process: Today felt like I was riding a wave of momentum I didn't expect. The World Cup match was a nice break from routine, and being around people cheering made me feel less isolated even though I was just watching. The transit closure was inconvenient, but walking gave me time to think instead of zoning out on the train. I think I used basketball as an escape from the guilt of not studying enough in the morning—physical activity always resets my brain. The late library session was me being anxious about my coursework, probably overcompensating. I'm noticing I oscillate between pushing hard and collapsing, and today was a compressed version of that cycle. Good day overall, but I need to find more balance.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District for maintenance until 3pm.
